authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
USDA Forest Service, Humboldt-Toiyabe National Forest (Carson Ranger District)

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Lower Sunset Lake not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Lower Sunset Lake not listed)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW (Table 1, Alpine County section, p.9-10)
Lower Sunset Lake Topo Map — Alpine County, CA (Pacific Valley Area) — TopoZone
Lower Sunset Lake (GNIS Feature ID 263047) — Alpine County, CA — HomeTownLocator GNIS Feature Detail
sources.quotedText: Lower Sunset Lake ... a physical feature (Lake) in Alpine County. It lies at 38.6114615 (North), -119.8752427 (West), matching the file's recorded coordinate (-119.8753, 38.6115) to within ~10m. Note: gaz_id 263047 was confirmed via HomeTownLocator's GNIS Feature Detail Report; it is absent from the carto.nationalmap.gov ArcGIS gazetteer mirror (a gap in that mirror's id sequence — ids 263045,263046,263049,263050 are present but 263047/263048 are not), which is why this record could not be fetched from the ArcGIS REST endpoint directly.

sources.title: Fishing at Lower Sunset Lake, California — FishAngler
sources.quotedText: Lower Sunset Lake is located in Alpine County, California.

sources.title: Blue Lakes and Pleasant Valley — Sierra Fly Fishing Guide
sources.quotedText: Sunset Lakes (elev: 7863 feet) about two mile from Tamarack Lake, a dirt road available requiring high clearance. Lahontan Cutthroats and Brookies.

sources.title: Blue Lakes — Carson Pass
sources.quotedText: There are many short, interconnecting OHV routes available out toward Tamarack Lake, the cluster of Sunset Lakes, and Wet Meadow Reservoir.

parameters.note: Lower Sunset Lake is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
